title: Arrays
description: Master Arrays from beginner to advanced.

patterns.name: Traversal
patterns.description: Learn to iterate through arrays efficiently to find, count, update, compare, and manipulate elements. Traversal is the foundation for almost every array problem.

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array

patterns.name: Frequency Array
patterns.description: Count occurrences of elements using arrays or hash maps. This pattern is frequently used in counting, duplicates, anagrams and missing number problems.

problems.title: First Unique Character in a String
Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array

patterns.name: Counting
patterns.description: Problems that require counting occurrences, consecutive values, or maintaining running counts.

problems.title: X of a Kind in a Deck of Cards

patterns.name: Simulation
patterns.description: Simulate the operations exactly as described in the problem. These problems focus on implementing the process rather than discovering a mathematical shortcut.

patterns.name: Rotation
patterns.description: Problems involving rotating arrays or matrices either left, right, or by a given number of positions.

patterns.name: In-place Modification
patterns.description: Modify arrays without using additional memory. Frequently asked in interviews due to space optimization.

problems.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II

patterns.name: Sorting Applications
patterns.description: Sorting is often used as preprocessing to simplify problems involving intervals, duplicates, greediness and ordering.

problems.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array

patterns.name: Two Pointers
patterns.description: Use two indices moving through the array to reduce time complexity from O(n²) to O(n). One of the most important interview patterns.

problems.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II
Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted

patterns.name: Sliding Window
patterns.description: Sliding Window optimizes problems involving contiguous subarrays or substrings by maintaining a moving window instead of recalculating every range.

problems.title: Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters
Find All Anagrams in a String
Replace the Substring for Balanced String
Number of Sub-arrays of Size K and Average Greater than or Equal to Threshold
Maximum Points You Can Obtain from Cards
Maximum Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length
Longest Subarray of 1's After Deleting One Element
Minimum Operations to Reduce X to Zero
Frequency of the Most Frequent Element
Maximize the Confusion of an Exam

patterns.name: Prefix Sum
patterns.description: Prefix Sum enables efficient range sum queries and transforms O(n²) subarray problems into O(n). It is one of the highest-frequency interview patterns.
commonMistakes: Using Sliding Window for negative numbers

problems.title: Sum of All Odd Length Subarrays

patterns.name: Difference Array
patterns.description: Difference Array efficiently handles multiple range update operations by updating only the boundaries instead of every element.

patterns.name: Binary Search
patterns.description: Binary Search is one of the highest-frequency interview topics. Learn searching in sorted arrays and variants.

problems.title: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array
Search in Rotated Sorted Array II
Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array II

patterns.name: Binary Search on Answer
patterns.description: Binary Search is applied on the answer space instead of array indices. One of the most important product-company interview patterns.

problems.title: Capacity To Ship Packages Within D Days
Find the Smallest Divisor Given a Threshold
Minimum Number of Days to Make m Bouquets
Minimum Limit of Balls in a Bag

patterns.name: Kadane's Algorithm
patterns.description: Kadane's Algorithm finds the maximum or minimum sum contiguous subarray in linear time. It is one of the most frequently asked interview patterns.

problems.title: Maximum Absolute Sum of Any Subarray

patterns.name: Intervals
patterns.description: Interval problems involve merging, inserting, comparing, and scheduling ranges. These are very common in coding interviews.

problems.title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons

patterns.name: Matrix
patterns.description: Matrix problems combine array traversal with two-dimensional indexing, simulation, searching, and graph-like thinking.

patterns.name: Greedy on Arrays
patterns.description: Make the locally optimal choice at every step to achieve a globally optimal solution. Many scheduling and optimization problems use this approach.

problems.title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons

patterns.name: Monotonic Queue
patterns.description: Maintain a deque in increasing or decreasing order to answer sliding window maximum/minimum queries efficiently.

problems.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K
Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff Less Than or Equal to Limit

patterns.name: Coordinate Compression
patterns.description: Compress large values into smaller ranks while preserving order. Useful for Fenwick Tree, Segment Tree and offline processing.

problems.title: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self

patterns.name: Bucket Sort
patterns.description: Use buckets or frequency arrays to solve ordering and frequency problems in linear time.

patterns.name: Cyclic Sort
patterns.description: Place every element at its correct index. This pattern is extremely useful for missing number and duplicate number problems.

problems.title: Find All Duplicates in an Array
Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
